title: Racy Irish Chicken Skewers
description: Spicy, crispy chicken skewers with a gochugaru kick, cooked sous‑vide for tender juiciness and finished in a quick sear for crunch – a crowd‑pleasing, low‑fat Irish‑fusion dish.
instructions: Trim and cut the chicken breast fillets into 2.5 cm cubes and season them with salt, pepper, and gochugaru.
Place the seasoned chicken in a vacuum‑sealed bag and vacuum seal.
Preheat a sous‑vide water bath to 65°C (149°F) and cook the chicken for 60 minutes.
While the chicken is cooking, mix honey, soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, and lemon juice in a small bowl to create a glaze.
After 60 minutes, remove the bag, pat the chicken dry with paper towels, and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Heat a non‑stick grill pan or skillet over high heat and add the olive oil, then sear the chicken cubes for 1–2 minutes per side until a golden crust forms.
Drizzle the glaze over the seared chicken and toss to coat, then transfer the cubes onto pre‑fried wooden skewers.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately with a side of roasted Irish potatoes or a light green salad.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
